English.doctor_signs:
• Soaking through a pad/tampon every hour for several hours
• Bleeding that lasts more than 8 days
• Severe cramps that don't improve with pain relief
• Fever (38°C/100°F) with foul-smelling discharge
• Severe headache or vision changes
• Sudden irregular periods after being regular
• Extreme fatigue, pale palms/dizziness (signs of anemia)
• Missed period for 3 months (not pregnant)

English.local_african_foods: 🌿 Local African Foods for Cycle Health
English.local_foods_text:
- **Moringa** (leaves): Rich in iron & calcium. Add to soups or stews. Helps prevent anemia.
- **Kontomire** (cocoyam leaves): High in iron. Great in stews.
- **Groundnuts (peanuts)**: Magnesium for cramps. Eat as snack or in soups.
- **Fonio**: Complex carbs for luteal phase. Gluten-free, easy to digest.
- **Palm oil** (red oil): Vitamin E for skin health during hormonal changes.
- **Okra**: Contains folate and fiber. Good for overall reproductive health.
- **Black-eyed peas**: Iron and protein. Help with energy during period.
English.local_remedies_text:
- **Ginger tea:** Grate fresh ginger, steep in hot water. Reduces cramps and nausea.
- **Moringa powder:** Add to meals. Boosts iron and energy.
- **Warm shea butter massage:** Gently massage on lower belly to soothe pain.
- **Warm salt compress:** Heat salt in a pan, wrap in cloth, place on belly.
- **Neem leaf wash:** Boil neem leaves, use water to wash external area – antibacterial.
- **Bitter kola:** Chew a small piece to reduce nausea (use sparingly).
- **Soursop leaves tea:** Consult an elder before using – claimed to reduce pain.
English.anemia_warning: You may have anemia signs. Eat iron-rich foods (moringa, beans, greens) and consider a blood test.

Hausa.doctor_signs:
• Soaking through a pad/tampon every hour for several hours
• Bleeding that lasts more than 8 days
• Severe cramps that don't improve with pain relief
• Fever (38°C/100°F) with foul-smelling discharge
• Severe headache or vision changes
• Sudden irregular periods after being regular
• Extreme fatigue, pale palms/dizziness (signs of anemia)
• Missed period for 3 months (not pregnant)

Hausa.local_foods_text:
- **Moringa** (ganye): Mai arzikin ƙarfe & calcium. Ƙara a miya. Yana taimakawa hana rashin jini.
- **Kontomire** (ganyen kwalekwale): Mai yawan ƙarfe. Yana da kyau a miya.
- **Gyada**: Magnesium don ciwon ciki. Ci a matsayin kayan ciye-ciye ko a miya.
- **Fonio**: Carbohydrate mai rikitarwa don lokacin luteal. Babu gluten, mai sauƙin narkewa.
- **Man dabino** (man ja): Vitamin E don lafiyar fata yayin canje-canjen hormone.
- **Kubewa**: Ya ƙunshi folate da fiber. Yana da kyau ga lafiyar haihuwa gabaɗaya.
- **Wake baƙar ido**: Ƙarfe da furotin. Yana taimakawa kuzari yayin haila.

Hausa.local_remedies_text:
- **Shayin ginger:** Gurje ginger mai sabo, jika a ruwan zafi. Yana rage ciwon ciki da tashin zuciya.
- **Foda na moringa:** Ƙara a abinci. Yana ƙara ƙarfe da kuzari.
- **Tausashin man shea mai dumi:** Tausasa ƙananan ciki don kwantar da zafi.
- **Danshin gishiri mai dumi:** Dumama gishiri a kasko, nannade a zane, sanya a ciki.
- **Wankin ganyen neem:** Tafasa ganyen neem, yi amfani da ruwan don wanke waje – mai kashe ƙwayoyin cuta.
- **Bitter kola:** Tauna ƙaramin yanki don rage tashin zuciya (yi amfani da shi kaɗan).
- **Shayin ganyen soursop:** Tuntuɓi dattijo kafin amfani – an yi iƙirarin yana rage zafi.
